LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS CARMINE AMOROSO AND ADA AMOROSO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BASED ON THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF PLAINTIFF, MCMANUS AND THE PROVISIONS OF BUILDING CODE SECTION 1001.1 AND THE HOUSING CODE SECTION 401. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE BUILDING WAS NOT REQUIRED TO HAVE 2 HANDRAILS. THERE WAS A HANDRAIL ON THE RIGHT SIDE THAT PLAINTIFF CHOSE NOT TO USE AND THE FALL WAS CAUSED BY THE GARBAGE BAG STRIKING PLAINTIFF'S LEG. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE THAT THE STAIRS WERE A DANGEROUS CONDITION. PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CCP 437 C(B)(3) OR CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1350. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS ARE NOT DIRECTED TO EVIDENCE BUT TO PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT HAS NOT RULED ON THE OBJECTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Off Calendar Oct-30-2008 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Eric Steinle of LaMore Brazier Riddle & Giampaoli (408.280.6800) for defendants and moving parties

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS CARMINE AMOROSO AND ADA AMOROSO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence based on the deposition testimony of plaintiff, McManus and the provisions of Building Code section 1001.1 and the Housing Code section 401. Plaintiff fails to present evidence that raises a triable issue of fact. The building was not required to have 2 handrails. There was a handrail on the right side that plaintiff chose not to use and the fall was caused by the garbage bag striking plaintiff's leg. Plaintiff has presented no evidence that the stairs were a dangerous condition. Plaintiff's response to defendant's separate statement does not comply with CCP 437 c(b)(3) or California Rule of Court 3.1350. Defendant's objections are not directed to evidence but to plaintiff's argument in the separate statement. Accordingly, the Court has not ruled on the objections. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
